title: COMPAT — NAICS 518210 (Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services)

sections.title: Verdict Summary
sections.content:
| # | Constraint | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Aqueous Media Requirement | none | CDU secondary loop uses water or PG/EG25 glycol — fits aqueous + ≤50% glycol envelope. |
| C2 | Maximum Medium Temperature (90 °C) | none | Facility CHW 14–27 °C; secondary TCS supply 20–45 °C (Rubin-class). Max ~45 °C is half the 90 °C limit. |
| C3 | Air Bubble / Void Fraction (2 % vol) | none | CDU loops are closed, de-aerated via expansion reservoir, and chemically treated. Void fraction stays well below 2 %. |
| C4 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | none | CDU loops run steady-state with slow setpoint ramps. Integrated NTC compensation handles the regime; ~0.2 % accuracy is adequate for ΔT × flow kW duty. |
| C5 | Wetted Material Chemical Compatibility | none | HVAC-grade treated water + PG/EG glycol + standard corrosion inhibitor — compatible with polymer housing and KTW/WRAS seals. |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition | none | CDU secondary is a closed, pressurized loop — always full by design. |
| C7 | Minimum Flow Rate (4.2 l/min DN20) | none | Rack secondary loop flows 5–30 l/min — above the DN20 lower detection limit. |
| C8 | Maximum Operating Pressure (10 bar) | none | CDU secondary loops run 2–6 bar — well within the 10 bar rating. |
| C9 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run (5–10 × DN) | **mitigable** | CDU skids are compact and pre-plumbed with elbows near the sensor port. Installation guidance or a short flow conditioner resolves it. |
| C10 | Life Endurance Ceiling (3,000 m³) | **mitigable** | **Key issue.** At 20 l/min continuous 24/7, endurance = ~104 days; at 30 l/min, ~69 days. Data-center duty cycle burns the current sensor in weeks to months. Needs a higher-endurance variant or scheduled-replacement consumable model. |
| C11 | No Explosive Atmosphere Certification | none | Data halls are not classified hazardous areas. ATEX not required. |
| C12 | Ambient Temperature Range (−20 to +60 °C) | none | White-space ambient is 18–27 °C (ASHRAE A1/A2). Electronics and RAST 2.5 connector operate well within range. |

**Knockouts:** 0
**Mitigable:** 2 (C9 installation geometry, C10 life endurance)
**None:** 10
**Market status:** SURVIVES — proceed to fit scoring.

---

sections.title: Mitigable Detail
sections.content:
### C9 — Minimum Straight Pipe Run

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Mitigation actions** | (1) Publish CDU-specific installation guide with required upstream straight length, (2) offer a bolt-on flow-conditioner insert (honeycomb or perforated plate), (3) validate accuracy curve with 3 × DN upstream via factory calibration. |
| **Cost estimate** | low (<$10K per variant NRE; unit cost add <$5) |
| **Time estimate** | 2–4 months |

### C10 — Life Endurance Ceiling

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Mitigation actions** | (1) Develop industrial-duty variant with reinforced transducer bonding and upgraded seals targeting ≥30,000 m³ throughput (10× current), (2) reposition the current sensor as a scheduled-replacement consumable aligned with annual CDU service intervals, (3) alternatively, qualify a DCIM firmware model that tracks cumulative throughput and triggers proactive swap alerts before drift. |
| **Cost estimate** | high (>$100K — new variant NRE, qualification testing, possibly new transducer adhesive system) |
| **Time estimate** | 12–18 months for the hardware variant; 3–6 months for the consumable/alert path |

---

sections.title: Fit Read
sections.content:
The sensor's measurement envelope is a near-perfect match for CDU and RDHx secondary-loop conditions. Integrated ΔT sensing delivers built-in thermal-duty telemetry (flow × ΔT → kW) that DCIM/PUE calculations otherwise require two sensors for — this is a latent FP-extension advantage, not a constraint issue.

The only structural barrier is **C10 life endurance**. The 3,000 m³ ceiling was sized for residential/commercial HVAC intermittent duty; hyperscale liquid-cooling is 24/7 continuous. Without a higher-endurance variant or an explicit consumable-sensor commercial model, field replacement cadence becomes operationally painful. This is mitigable, not a knockout, but it is the dominant engineering and go-to-market question for this market.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- 01_product_decomposition.md — spec envelope (DN20, 4.2–46.6 l/min, 0–6 bar, +2 to 90 °C, 3,000 m³ endurance)
- 03_constraints.md — 12 constraints with thresholds and absolute/mitigable classification
- VN_data_processing_hosting.md — CDU/RDHx anchor, secondary-loop conditions (20–45 °C, 2–6 bar, 5–30 l/min, water/PG25)
- Tom's Hardware, Network World — 2025–26 liquid-cooling trend and Rubin-class supply temperatures

rows.Rationale: CDU secondary loop uses water or PG/EG25 glycol — fits aqueous + ≤50% glycol envelope.
Facility CHW 14–27 °C; secondary TCS supply 20–45 °C (Rubin-class). Max ~45 °C is half the 90 °C limit.
CDU loops are closed, de-aerated via expansion reservoir, and chemically treated. Void fraction stays well below 2 %.
CDU loops run steady-state with slow setpoint ramps. Integrated NTC compensation handles the regime; ~0.2 % accuracy is adequate for ΔT × flow kW duty.
HVAC-grade treated water + PG/EG glycol + standard corrosion inhibitor — compatible with polymer housing and KTW/WRAS seals.
CDU secondary is a closed, pressurized loop — always full by design.
Rack secondary loop flows 5–30 l/min — above the DN20 lower detection limit.
CDU secondary loops run 2–6 bar — well within the 10 bar rating.
CDU skids are compact and pre-plumbed with elbows near the sensor port. Installation guidance or a short flow conditioner resolves it.

**Key issue.** At 20 l/min continuous 24/7, endurance = ~104 days; at 30 l/min, ~69 days. Data-center duty cycle burns the current sensor in weeks to months. Needs a higher-endurance variant or scheduled-replacement consumable model.
Data halls are not classified hazardous areas. ATEX not required.
White-space ambient is 18–27 °C (ASHRAE A1/A2). Electronics and RAST 2.5 connector operate well within range.

rows.Value: (1) Publish CDU-specific installation guide with required upstream straight length, (2) offer a bolt-on flow-conditioner insert (honeycomb or perforated plate), (3) validate accuracy curve with 3 × DN upstream via factory calibration.
low (<$10K per variant NRE; unit cost add <$5)

(1) Develop industrial-duty variant with reinforced transducer bonding and upgraded seals targeting ≥30,000 m³ throughput (10× current), (2) reposition the current sensor as a scheduled-replacement consumable aligned with annual CDU service intervals, (3) alternatively, qualify a DCIM firmware model that tracks cumulative throughput and triggers proactive swap alerts before drift.
high (>$100K — new variant NRE, qualification testing, possibly new transducer adhesive system)
12–18 months for the hardware variant; 3–6 months for the consumable/alert path
